fact: Zealot factions inside Jerusalem burned the city's grain stores during the siege
source: Josephus, The Wars of the Jews, Book 5, Chapter 1

fact: Rival factions inside Jerusalem fought and killed each other during the siege
source: Josephus, The Wars of the Jews, Book 5, Chapters 1–3 (describing the three-way civil war between Simon bar Giora, John of Giscala, and Eleazar ben Simon)

fact: Four Roman legions besieged Jerusalem in 70 AD
source: Josephus, The Wars of the Jews, Book 5, Chapter 1; the legions were V Macedonica, X Fretensis, XII Fulminata, and XV Apollinaris under Titus

fact: Rome offered terms of surrender before the final destruction
source: Josephus, The Wars of the Jews, Book 5, Chapter 9 and Book 6, Chapter 2 (Titus sends Josephus himself to negotiate surrender)

fact: Severe famine inside Jerusalem during the siege, with graphic descriptions of starvation
source: Josephus, The Wars of the Jews, Book 5, Chapter 10 and Book 6, Chapter 3

fact: The Temple in Jerusalem was destroyed by Rome in 70 AD
source: Josephus, The Wars of the Jews, Book 6, Chapter 4; widely documented across ancient sources including Tacitus, Histories, Book 5
